title: Combat aerospace patrol
content:
{{Top|leg}}
'''Combat aerospace patrol''' was the term given to refer to the tactic of sending [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] to fly cover for ground-based forces while they conducted operations against a specified target. [[Pilot/Legends|Pilots]] engaged in this activity would watch for and attempt to intercept any enemy [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmospheric]] or [[Realspace/Legends|spacebased]] defenses that attempted to intervene, essentially covering the section of a [[Planet/Legends|planet's]] air and space immediately surrounding the area of land-based engagements.

==History==
The tactic was used throughout the [[Timeline of galactic history/Legends|history]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] by almost all military and [[Law enforcement agency/Legends|police]] organizations and governments that fielded starfighter or [[Airspeeder/Legends|airspeeder]] forces, as the tactic was not exclusive to the former. During a raid on [[Kerilt]] by the [[Invids]] led by [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Leonia Tavira]], [[Corran Horn/Legends|Corran Horn]] in the guise of [[Jenos Idanian]], ordered elements of [[Bolt Squadron (Khuiumin Survivors)|Bolt]] to engage in combat aerospace patrol while he went dirtdown to search out a [[Flight group/Legends|flight]] of his pilots, led by [[Remart Sasyru]], who had gone missing while securing the [[Morymento]] warehouses.

==Appearances==
*''[[X-Wing: Rogue Squadron]]'' {{Ab|X-Wing: Rogue Squadron (unabridged audiobook)}}
*''[[I, Jedi]]'' {{Ab|I, Jedi (unabridged audiobook)}}
